events.title: Massacre of Bi'r Ma'una
events.significance: Part of the ayyām al-ʿarab (battle days of the Arabs)

events.title: Battle of Haydaran
events.significance: Major victory that established Hilalian dominance in the Maghreb

events.title: Battle of Setif
events.significance: Led to forced migration of many Hilalians to Morocco

participants.role: Together with the Bani Kaab, the Al Bu Shamis were ranged against British forces

events.title: Wars against Ottomans
events.description: Al Awazem were among the only tribes to have fought the Ottomans
events.significance: Demonstrated tribal independence and resistance to Ottoman control

events.title: Wars against Achaemenids (Iranians)
events.description: Al Awazem fought against Achaemenid (Iranian) forces
events.significance: Part of historical conflicts with Persian powers

events.title: Protection of Emirate of Diriyah
events.description: They once fought seven tribes to protect the Emirate of Diriyah
events.significance: Showed loyalty to the first Saudi state

events.title: Early settlement of Kuwait
events.description: Al Awazem were among the first tribal groups to inhabit Kuwait City
events.significance: Foundational role in Kuwait's tribal composition

events.title: Village Secessions from Ras Al Khaimah
events.description: Villages of Rams and Sha'am seceded several times from Ras Al Khaimah rule
events.significance: Demonstrates the political tensions between tribal groups and ruling authorities

participants.role: The Shihuh (including Bani Hadiyah) were involved in these secessions due to fractious relations with rulers

events.title: Establishment of Emirate of Mahamid
events.description: The Mahamid established an emirate in the Karak region with the majority from the Al-Amru clan ruling for a long time
events.significance: Established political control in Karak region, became dominant power

events.title: Fall of Emirate of Mahamid
events.description: Different clans united against the Mahamid due to their cruel rule, leading to the end of their emirate
events.significance: End of Mahamid political dominance in Karak region

events.title: Tribal confederation conflicts with Sasanian Lakhmids
events.description: Pre-Islamic conflicts involving tribal confederations including ancestors of the Kunud
events.significance: Foundational period for tribal identity

participants.role: The Kunud trace their origin to the tribal confederation that fought with the Sasanian's Lakhmid clients

events.title: Mahri Conversion to Islam
events.description: Delegation led by Mahri bin Al-Abid visited Prophet Muhammad and embraced Islam
events.significance: Established the tribe's Islamic faith and received Prophet's letter of protection

events.title: Sacking of Dubai by Abu Dhabi
events.description: Dubai was sacked by a force from Abu Dhabi under Khalifa bin Shakhbut Al Nahyan following the exodus and plague outbreak
events.significance: Major political upheaval in early Dubai history

events.title: 1819 British Punitive Expedition against Ras Al Khaimah
Migration from Sinniyah Island
events.description: The Al Ali tribe moved from the island of Sinniyah to the mainland after water supplies on the island were exhausted
events.significance: Led to the founding of Umm Al Quwain on the mainland

events.title: Al Thani Collection at Hôtel de la Marine
Establishment of Jubail
events.description: The Al-Buainain established the town of Jubail on the Hasa coast –northwest of Ras Tanura– following dissatisfaction in Qatar.
events.significance: Created a major settlement that became important for the tribe

events.title: Jewish Diaspora Name Variations
events.description: Through the Jewish diaspora, the Cohen surname acquired dozens of variants as multiple languages were acquired
events.significance: Led to the proliferation of Cohen surname variants across different cultures and languages

events.title: 1972 Sharjawi coup attempt by Saqr bin Sultan Al Qasimi
Al Nahyan Migration to Abu Dhabi
Establishment of Abu Dhabi Islands Archaeological Survey
Establishment of Temple Priesthood
events.description: The founding of the hereditary priestly caste serving in the Temple of Jerusalem, with Aaron as the first Kohen
events.significance: Established the hereditary basis for the Cohen lineage and priestly duties in Judaism

events.title: Early Iranian Migration to Bahrain
events.description: Iranian migration to Bahrain traced back to Achaemenid and Sassanid Persian Empire periods
events.significance: Establishes earliest documented Iranian presence in Bahrain

events.title: Conflict with Himyar
events.description: Himyar overpowered Kahlan tribes and forced them out of Yemeni highlands
events.significance: Major turning point that led to Kahlan diaspora throughout Arabia

events.title: Tayy Invasion of Ajaa and Salma Mountains
events.description: Tayy branch led by Usma bin Luai invaded and took control of mountains from Banu Assad and Tamim
events.significance: Established Tayy presence in northern Arabia

events.title: Himyarite Independence from Qataban
events.description: Himyar gained independence from the Qatabanian kingdom
events.significance: Marked the beginning of Himyar as an independent kingdom

events.title: First Conquest of Saba'
events.description: Himyar conquered neighboring Saba' for the first time
events.significance: Beginning of Himyarite expansion and dominance in southern Arabia

events.title: UAE Formation
events.description: The text provided does not contain information about UAE Formation. Instead, it discusses two geological formations in Colombia: the Une Formation (Middle Cretaceous sandstone formation) and the Usme Formation (Neogene-Paleogene formation). No political or historical event related to the United Arab Emirates formation is mentioned.
events.significance: The query appears to be mismatched with the source material, which focuses on Colombian geological formations rather than UAE political history
events.outcome: No relevant information found about UAE Formation in the provided text
events.surpriseFactor: The complete absence of UAE-related content in a text about Colombian geology

events.title: Tayy Migration from Yemen
events.description: Led by Usma bin Luai, the Tayy invaded mountains of Ajā and Salma from Banu Assad and Banu Tamim
events.significance: Established Shammar presence in northern Arabian Peninsula

events.title: Collapse of Ma'rib Dam
events.description: The collapse of the Ma'rib Dam forced the Azd to emigrate from Yemen
events.significance: Triggered the great Azdi migration and dispersal across the Arabian Peninsula

events.title: Battle of Salut
events.description: Conflict between Malik ibn Fehm's Azdite men and the Persians who claimed Oman's territory
events.significance: Decisive battle that led to Persian defeat and paved the way for Arabization of Oman

events.title: Destruction of Marib Dam
events.description: Destruction of the Marib Dam forced Kahlani tribes to emigrate northwards
events.significance: Catalyst for major Kahlani migration throughout Arabian Peninsula

events.title: Conversion to Judaism
events.description: Judaism adopted as de facto state religion under Malkikarib Yuhamin
events.significance: Major religious shift from South Arabian polytheism to Judaism

events.title: Mudar Chief Meets Himyarite King
events.description: The earliest text that mentions Mudar is an inscription, al-῾Irāfa 1 (c. 440?), discovered at Zafar, the capital of the Kingdom of Himyar. The inscription mentions the chief of Mudar travelling to Mas'al Jumh to meet the king of Himyar.
events.significance: Demonstrates Mudar was under Himyarite hegemony at this time

events.title: Mudar Rise to Power in Hejaz
events.description: Mudar rose to power over the Hejaz in the fifth century, after supplanting its predecessor, the Banu Ghassan, who migrated to Syria.
events.significance: Marked Mudar's emergence as the dominant power in the Hejaz region

events.title: Himyarite Expedition with Mudar Reinforcements
events.description: Maʾsal 2, dated to June of 521 AD, mentions a Himyarite expedition into Sasanian territory which received reinforcements from Mudar, and its leaders, the Banu Tha'labah.
events.significance: Shows Mudar's military involvement in Himyarite campaigns against Sassanians

events.title: Fall of Himyar
events.description: Fall of Himyar led to decline of Kinda branches that were vassal kingdoms
events.significance: Marked end of Kindite power in Arabia

events.title: Aksumite Conquest of Himyar
events.description: Kingdom of Aksum conquered Himyar, ending independent Himyarite rule
events.significance: End of Himyarite independence and beginning of foreign rule

events.title: Defense against Abraha's Cavalry
events.description: When Abraha the Abyssinian passed by Azd lands heading to destroy Kaaba, he sent cavalry to the Azd, but Ghamid confronted and defeated them
events.significance: Early demonstration of Ghamid military prowess in defense of their territory

events.title: Sasanian Annexation
events.description: Himyar annexed as province by Sasanian Empire
events.significance: Final end of Himyarite political entity

events.title: Fijar War
events.description: War between Quraysh and Kinana against various Qaysi tribes including Hawazin over control of trade routes
events.significance: Established Quraysh control over western Arabian trade routes

events.title: The Hijrah
events.description: Muhammad and his followers migrated to Medina after negotiations with Banu Aws and Banu Khazraj
events.significance: Marked the beginning of armed conflict between Muslims and Quraysh

events.title: Battle of Badr
events.description: Major battle between Muslim forces and Quraysh
events.significance: Part of escalating conflict from caravan raids

events.title: Battle of Uhud
events.description: Major battle between Muslim forces and Quraysh
events.significance: Continuation of armed conflict with Muslims

events.title: Expedition of Muhammad ibn Maslamah
events.description: Muhammad ordered military expedition against Banu Hanifa sept, resulting in capture of their chief Thumamah
events.significance: Led to conversion of tribal chief to Islam

events.title: Treaty of al-Hudaybiya
events.description: Ten-year truce formalized between Quraysh and Muhammad, allowing Muslims to perform Umrah
events.significance: Marked shift toward conciliation, broken approximately two years later leading to conquest of Mecca

events.title: First Delegation to Prophet Muhammad in Mecca
events.description: Abi Dhibyan led delegation to Prophet Muhammad, received document guaranteeing tribal rights upon accepting Islam
events.significance: Early conversion to Islam and formal recognition of tribal rights

events.title: Conquest of Mecca
events.description: Muhammad marched with 10,000 men to besiege Mecca after treaty was broken, city surrendered virtually unopposed
events.significance: Almost all Quraysh converted to Islam, establishing Muslim control over Mecca

events.title: Bloodwit case cancellation
events.description: Prophet Muhammad cancelled Rabi'ah ibn al-Harith's bloodwit claim, making it the first such case to be abolished
events.significance: Symbolic first case in Islamic legal reform

events.title: Battle of Hunayn
events.significance: Marked the tribe's conversion to Islam

participants.role: Likely accepted Islam after Muhammad's victory

events.title: First Contact with Prophet Muhammad
events.description: Banu Tamim came into contact with Muhammad in the 8th year of Hijrah but did not immediately convert to Islam
events.significance: Marked the beginning of the tribe's relationship with the new Islamic state

events.title: Arab Conquest and Islamic Conversion
events.description: Rab'iah tribe converted to Islam in 630 A.D.
events.significance: Major religious transformation of the indigenous population

events.title: Second Delegation to Prophet Muhammad in Madinah
events.description: Ten-man delegation came to Madinah, Prophet demonstrated miraculous knowledge of theft at their camp
events.significance: Strengthened faith through miraculous event, formal Islamic instruction received

events.title: Ridda Wars
events.description: Banu Hanifa apostatized under Musaylimah's leadership, fought against Medina Muslims
events.significance: Major apostasy movement that required three attempts by Muslims to defeat

events.title: Battle of Aqraba
events.description: Final battle where Musaylimah was killed and Banu Hanifa defeated
events.significance: End of the apostasy movement, tribe rejoined Islamic state

events.title: Conversion to Islam
events.description: The tribe eventually converted to Islam and participated in the Islamic conquests
events.significance: Became one of the major Arab tribes supporting Islamic expansion

events.title: Conquest of Fars and Makran
events.description: Muslim conquests of Fars and Makran dominated by Azdi tribes
events.significance: Established Azdi dominance in eastern Caliphate territories

events.title: Conquest of Homs
events.description: Himyarite descendants led South Arabian contingents in Muslim conquest of Homs
events.significance: Himyarite descendants established Homs as center of South Arabian power in Islamic Syria

events.title: Islamic Conquests of North Africa
events.description: Mahri forces participated in Arab Muslim conquests under Amr ibn al-As, fighting in battles of Heliopolis and Nikiou
events.significance: Established Mahri presence in North Africa and earned them lands in newly conquered territories

events.title: Battle of Siffin
events.description: The Anizah tribe participated with other Rabi'a tribes on the side of the fourth Rashidun caliph, Ali ibn Abi Talib
events.significance: Demonstrated Anizah's military strength and political alignment in early Islamic civil wars

participants.role: Allied with Ali ibn Abi Talib with 4,000 armored warriors

events.title: Azdi prominence in Basra
events.description: The Azdi 'Uman became especially prominent in Basra due to favors from Ziyad ibn Abihi
events.significance: Peak of Azdi political influence in early Islamic period

events.title: Battle of Marj Rahit
events.description: Himyarite descendants defeated by Quda'a confederation and Umayyad caliph Marwan I
events.significance: Marked the decline of Himyarite influence in Islamic Syria

events.title: Rabi'a migration to Egypt
events.description: Migration of Bani Hanifa and related Bakr tribesmen to southern Egypt during Abbasid era
events.significance: Established Rabi'a presence in Egypt and control of gold mines

events.title: Aghlabid Dynasty Rule
events.description: Banu Tamim established the Aghlabid dynasty ruling Ifriqiya from 800 to 909
events.significance: Controlled modern-day Tunisia, Algeria, Libya, and parts of southern Italy and Sicily

events.title: Qarmatian Uprising against Fatimids
events.significance: Led to their defeat and forced relocation to Upper Egypt

participants.role: Allied with Qarmatians against Fatimid Caliphate

events.title: Al Fadl assist against Crusaders
events.description: Al Fadl tribe participated in Muslim war effort against Crusaders who conquered Syrian coastal regions
events.significance: Established Al Fadl prominence in Syria through military service

events.title: Plague decimation of Anz tribe
events.description: Anz tribe was decimated by plague in the 13th century
events.significance: Major population loss for this Rabi'a sub-tribe

events.title: First appointment as Amir al-ʿarab
events.description: Sultan al-Adil appointed Haditha as amir al-ʿarab, giving Al Fadl command over Bedouin tribes
events.significance: Formalized Al Fadl authority over northern Syrian Bedouin tribes

events.title: Initial Huwala migration to Iran
events.description: Arabian Iranians of tribal lineage initially migrated to Iran in the 13th and 14th century and intermixed with the indigenous population
events.significance: Established Huwala presence in Southern Iran

events.title: Hereditary office established
events.description: Starting with Isa ibn Muhanna, Al Fadl became hereditary holders of amir al-ʿarab office under Mamluk sultans
events.significance: Secured long-term Al Fadl leadership and substantial territorial grants

events.title: Baloch Migration to Balochistan
events.description: The Baloch people entered the Balochistan region in the 14th century CE
events.significance: Marks the beginning of Baloch presence in the region

events.title: Mir Chakar Rind becomes First Sirdar
events.description: Mir Chakar Rind became the first Sirdar of Afghan, Iranian, and Pakistani Balochistan in the 15th century
events.significance: First unified Baloch leadership across the region

events.title: Attack on Otaiba in eastern Ta'if
events.description: Sharif of Mecca Muhammad ibn Barakat attacked Otaiba in eastern Ta'if
events.significance: Early documented conflict showing Otaibah presence in Hejaz region

events.title: Portuguese Sack of Khor Fakkan
events.description: Portuguese forces under Albuquerque sacked Khor Fakkan
events.significance: Demonstrates the long-standing presence of the tribe in the region

participants.role: The Naqbiyin were considered to have been present in Khor Fakkan prior to this attack

events.title: Ottoman preservation of leadership
events.description: Ottomans preserved Al Fadl's hereditary leadership of Bedouin tribes
events.significance: Continuity of Al Fadl authority under new imperial power

events.title: Ethiopian-Adal War
events.description: Mahri participated in 16th century conflict on the side of Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Ghazi
events.significance: Demonstrates their historical involvement in Horn of Africa affairs

participants.role: supporters of Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Ghazi

events.title: Al Fadl tribal division
events.description: Leading emirs joined Mawali tribe (became Al Abu Risha), while rivals were driven to Beqaa Valley
events.significance: Major division within the tribe affecting territorial control

events.title: Zaid bin Mohsen Invasion
events.description: Zaid bin Mohsen invaded Ghamid territory, ended in reconciliation
events.significance: Territorial conflict resolved through diplomacy

events.title: Mutayr Eastward Migration
events.description: Large-scale migration of Mutayr tribe from northern Hejaz eastwards into central Arabia
events.significance: Major demographic shift that displaced other Bedouin tribes and established Mutayr dominance in central Arabia

events.title: Battle against Banu Al-Harith in Bisha
events.description: Assisted Khath'am against Banu Al-Harith, led by Abu Dhahiba with 5 battalions
events.significance: Demonstrated inter-tribal cooperation despite previous conflicts

events.title: Founding of Bani Khalid Emirate
events.description: Emir Barrak ibn Ghurayr established the Bani Khalid Emirate with capital in Al-Mubarraz
events.significance: Marked the beginning of Bani Khalid political dominance in eastern Arabia

events.title: Expulsion of Ottoman Forces
events.description: Bani Khalid forces under Barrak ibn Ghurayr expelled Ottoman forces from eastern Arabian cities and towns
events.significance: Established Bani Khalid independence from Ottoman control

events.title: Battle of 'Asir
events.description: Sharif of Mecca with Ghamid knights invaded and subjugated Asir regions
events.significance: Expansion of Sharif influence with Ghamid military support

events.title: Battle of Ranyah against Subay'
events.description: Border dispute led to full tribal mobilization, ended in Ghamid victory
events.significance: Successful territorial defense and tribal prestige

events.title: Battle against Al-Shalawi Tribe
events.description: Response to attacks on Ghamid commercial convoys to Mecca, ended in Ghamid victory
events.significance: Protection of trade routes and commercial interests

events.title: Battle of Al-Dhafa'a
events.description: Ghamid knights served under Sharif Muhammad Al-Harith against Dhafir tribes in Al-Qassim
events.significance: Major victory expelling Dhafir from Najd and Al-Qassim

events.title: Battle of Hadiya
events.description: Sharif with Ghamid and Thaqif attacked Bani Khalid, killed Sheikh Saqan bin Khalaf
events.significance: Significant victory against major tribal confederation

events.title: Invasion of Oman
events.description: Safavids requested Utub assistance in invading Oman but were defeated as they were engaged in war with Ottomans
events.significance: Early example of Utub military involvement in regional conflicts

events.title: Arrival of Banu Utbah
events.description: Banu Utbah (ancestors of Bahraini royal family) arrived in Bahrain in 18th century
events.significance: Political power shift affecting indigenous Baharna status

events.title: Sharif Saad vs Abdul Karim Conflict
events.description: Ghamid supported Sharif Saad bin Zaid against Sharif Abdul Karim, resulted in victory
events.significance: Involvement in Meccan political succession disputes

events.title: Omani invasion of Bahrain
events.description: Utub assisted Sultan bin Saif II in successfully taking Bahrain from the Safavids
events.significance: Established Utub involvement in Bahrain affairs

events.title: Na'im support for Imam Yarub
events.description: The Na'im followed Ghafiri tradition and supported Omani Imam Yarub in his bid to control Oman
events.significance: Demonstrates Na'im alignment with Ghafiri political faction

events.title: Habus Resistance to Taxation
events.description: The Habus successfully resisted an attempt by Sheikh Sultan bin Salim Al Qasimi to impose taxation on them
events.significance: Demonstrated Habus independence and led to formal agreement between the tribes

events.title: Settlement in Ras Al Khaimah
events.description: Al Bu Muhair first settled in Ras Al Khaimah from Abu Dhabi with the establishment of Al Qawasim rule
events.significance: Established significant presence in northern Emirates under Qasimi protection

events.title: Otaibah dominance in central Najd
events.description: Otaibah superseded Mutayr and Qahtan to become the largest tribe in central Najd
events.significance: Established Otaibah as the dominant tribal power in central Arabian Peninsula

events.title: Formation of Albu Salih Confederation
events.description: Albu Salih transformed from traditional tribe into tribal confederation (warfare alliance) in opposition to Ottoman Empire influence
events.significance: This transformation allowed them to reduce internal conflicts and unite neighboring clans under their leadership

events.title: Mazrui Rule of Mombasa
events.description: The Mazrui governed Mombasa and other coastal places in the 18th century
events.significance: Established Mazrui political dominance in East African coast

events.title: Mazrui-Portuguese Alliance Against Zanzibar
events.description: The Mazrui attacked Stone Town allying with the Portuguese against the Al Bu Sa'id
events.significance: Shows Mazrui opposition to Omani Al Bu Sa'id rule

events.title: First Saudi Attacks
events.description: Saudi forces led by Abdulaziz bin Muhammad launched first attacks against Bani Khalid
events.significance: Beginning of prolonged Saudi-Khalidi conflict

events.title: Construction of Umm Al Quwain Fort
events.description: Sheikh Rashid bin Majid Al Mualla was responsible for the construction of Umm Al Quwain Fort in the town, today home to Umm Al Quwain museum
events.significance: Marked the establishment of Umm Al Quwain as a permanent settlement

events.title: Zand recognition of Qasimi rule
events.description: Iranian Zand dynasty acknowledged and recognized a Qasimi as local ruler (farmandar) of Bandar Lengeh
events.significance: Formalized Qasimi control over southern Iranian coasts

events.title: Persian-Bahrain conflict
events.description: Al-Dhafeer provided cavalry and fighters to help Sheikh Ahmed bin Muhammad bin Khalifa expel Persian rule from Bahrain
events.significance: Decisive victory in Battle of Zubarah ending Persian control

participants.role: Allied forces supporting House of Khalifa

events.title: Al Khalifa Conquest of Bahrain
events.description: Al Khalifa conquered Bahrain from the Persians.

events.title: Bani Utbah conquest of Bahrain
events.description: Decisive conquest that established permanent Utub rule under Al-Khalifa
events.significance: Established lasting Al-Khalifa rule over Bahrain that continues today

events.title: Al-Buainain Migration to Bahrain
events.description: The Al-Buainain tribe was one of several bedouin tribes to move to Bahrain after the Al Khalifa conquered the island.
events.significance: Established their presence in Bahrain and the Gulf region

events.title: Saudi Conquest of Al-Hasa
events.description: Saudi forces conquered Al-Hasa and deposed the Al 'Ura'yir rulers from Bani Khalid
events.significance: Major loss of territory and power for Bani Khalid

events.title: Abu Dhabi Island Settlement
events.description: Bani Yas migrated to Abu Dhabi island after discovery of fresh water.

events.title: Founding of Abu Dhabi Town
events.description: The Al Bu Falah settled on Abu Dhabi island and established the town, which became the seat of the Emirate with the construction of Qasr al-Hosn
events.significance: Established the foundation of modern Abu Dhabi

events.title: Acquisition of Wadi Naqab
events.description: Habus gradually bought up Wadi Naqab territory from the displaced Naqbiyin tribe
events.significance: Expanded Habus agricultural holdings and territorial control

events.title: Al Sharqi Independence Attempts
events.description: The Sharqiyin tribe made several attempts over the centuries to secede from Sharjah and declare independence
events.significance: Led to eventual independence of Fujairah

events.title: Major Iranian Migration Wave
events.description: Large-scale migration from southern Iranian provinces including Fars, Bushehr, Hormozgan, and others
events.significance: Formed the basis of the modern Ajam community in Bahrain

events.title: British-Qawasim War (1809)
events.description: British attack on Qawasim, part of the 'Pirate Wars'.

events.title: Battle of Khakeekera
events.description: Victory against Rahmah ibn Jabir al-Jalhami who was preparing to invade Bahrain
events.significance: Defended Utub control of Bahrain against Wahhabi-allied forces

events.title: Battle of Quraish Valley
events.description: Ghamid army led by Salih bin Habash defeated Turkish army of Abdin Bey (20,000 fighters)
events.significance: Major victory against Ottoman-Egyptian forces during Wahhabi-Ottoman wars

events.title: Conquest of Ajman
events.description: Sheikh Rashid bin Humaid Al Nuaimi and fifty followers took the coastal settlement of Ajman from the Al Bu Shamis tribe
events.significance: Established Al Nuaimi rule over Ajman emirate

events.title: Alliance with Ibrahim Mohammed Ali
events.description: Ibrahim Mohammed Ali persuaded Otaibah and Anazzah tribes to assist against Abdullah bin Saud after defeating Wahhabi kingdom
events.significance: Demonstrated Otaibah's strategic importance in regional power struggles

events.title: Conquest of Ajman
events.description: Sheikh Rashid bin Humaid Al Nuaimi and fifty followers took the coastal settlement of Ajman from the Al Bu Shamis tribe
events.significance: Established Al Nuaimi rule over Ajman emirate

events.title: Fall of Ajman Fort
events.description: The Ajman fort finally fell to Rashid's followers and his rule was endorsed by Sheikh Sultan bin Saqr Al Qasimi
events.significance: Consolidated Al Nuaimi control over Ajman

events.title: Fall of Ajman Fort
events.description: The Ajman fort finally fell to Rashid's followers and his rule was endorsed by Sheikh Sultan bin Saqr Al Qasimi
events.significance: Consolidated Al Nuaimi control and gained recognition from neighboring rulers

events.title: Ottoman Reinstatement
events.description: Ottomans reinstated members of Al 'Uray'ir as rulers after conquering Al-Hasa and Al-Qatif
events.significance: Brief restoration of Bani Khalid authority under Ottoman patronage

events.title: British-Qawasim War (1819)
events.description: Major British campaign against Qawasim. Led to 1820 treaty and 'Trucial Coast' naming.

events.title: General Treaty of Peace (1820)
events.description: Treaty between Britain and the Trucial sheikhdoms after the Qawasim wars.

events.title: 1820 Treaty Signing
events.description: Rajib bin Ahmed Al Zaabi signed the original 1820 treaty between the Trucial States and the British as one of four independent signatories
events.significance: Established the Zaab tribe's political recognition and independence before later incorporation into Ras Al Khaimah

participants.role: Independent signatory representing Jazirat Al Hamra

events.title: Al Maktoum Takeover of Dubai
events.description: The House of Al Maktoum took over Dubai, a coastal trade city and established themselves as their own Emirate of Dubai
events.significance: Established Dubai as separate emirate under Bani Yas leadership

events.title: Battle with Tribal Alliance
events.description: Decisive defeat by alliance led by Mutayr and Ajman tribes
events.significance: Major step toward end of Bani Khalid rule

events.title: Sharjah Agreement of 1824
events.description: Sheikh Sultan bin Saqr Al Qasimi of Sharjah recognized Tahnun Al Nahyan's claim to Buraimi and demolished the forts he had built there
events.significance: Established Al Nahyan primacy over the Dhawahir area

events.title: Establishment of Pearl Trading Business
events.description: Salem Bin Darwish Al Mannai established the pearl trading business in Bahrain
events.significance: Beginning of Al Mannai commercial prominence in Gulf region

events.title: Battle of Al Shimasiyah
events.description: Sheikh Mashaan Ibn Hathal led the Bedouin clans of the Anizah tribe in a unifying battle against the tribe's enemies. Though Sheikh Mashaan died in the battle, the Anizah won.
events.significance: Marked a significant moment of tribal unity and military victory for Anizah

participants.role: Led by Sheikh Mashaan Ibn Hathal, unified Anizah clans against enemies

events.title: Final Defeat by Subay' and Al Saud
events.description: Final battle with Subay' tribe and Al Saud that ended Bani Khalid rule
events.significance: Definitive end of Bani Khalid political power

events.title: Dubai Split from Abu Dhabi
events.description: Maktoum bin Butti led ~800 Bani Yas members to settle Dubai, splitting from Abu Dhabi due to factional disputes.

events.title: Conquest of Mombasa
events.description: Al Bin Ali under Isa bin Tarif captured Fort Jesus with Omani support
events.significance: Demonstrated Utub maritime reach extending to East Africa

events.title: Abu Dhabi Raid on Dubai
events.description: Khalifa bin Shakhbut of Abu Dhabi raided Dubai during pearling season, attacking watchtower
events.significance: Early conflict over Dubai's secession from Bani Yas confederation

events.title: Dhawahir Inclusion Agreement
events.description: Sheikh Khalifa bin Shakbut Al Nahyan took full responsibility for the Bani Yas, Manasir and, for the first time, the Dhawahir
events.significance: Formalized Dhawahir allegiance to Abu Dhabi

events.title: Al Bu Muhair exodus from Dubai
events.description: 500 members of Al Bu Muhair left Dubai dissatisfied with Maktoum bin Butti's rule and settled in Sharjah
events.significance: Led to temporary abandonment of Dubai and contributed to its subsequent sacking by Abu Dhabi forces

events.title: Conflict with Khalifa bin Shakhbut Al Nahyan
events.description: The Ghafalah found themselves at odds with Khalifa bin Shakhbut Al Nahyan of Abu Dhabi in 1843. Khalifa went to war against the Bani Qitab, Na'im and Ghafalah, raiding the interior and looting Dhaid oasis
events.significance: Part of broader tribal conflicts following the 1833 founding of Dubai

events.title: Battle with Maktoum bin Butti
events.description: The Ghafalah hit back at Maktoum bin Butti in November 1843 and he attacked them in turn, putting them to flight but losing his eye in the conflict
events.significance: Notable for Maktoum losing his eye in the battle

events.title: 1843 Maritime Truce
events.description: Signed the ten-year Maritime Truce with British, precursor to Perpetual Maritime Truce of 1853
events.significance: Established formal relationship with British and maritime peace framework

events.title: Buraimi Alliance Against Wahhabis
events.description: The Bani Kaab allied with Sheikh Khalifa bin Shakhbut Al Nayhan of the Bani Yas in a tribal confederation to drive Wahhabi forces from Buraimi
events.significance: Major regional alliance against Wahhabi expansion

events.title: 1847 Engagement to Prohibit Slave Trade
events.description: Signed treaty to abolish slave trade and allow right of search
events.significance: Part of broader British efforts to eliminate slave trade in the Gulf

events.title: Buraimi Wahhabi Campaign
events.description: The Awamir helped Sheikh Saeed capture back his two forts from Wahhabis encamped in Buraimi
events.significance: Part of decisive strategic victory that cleared Buraimi of Wahhabi forces by 1850

participants.role: supported Sheikh Saeed bin Tahnun Al Nahyan against Wahhabi forces

events.title: Anti-Wahhabi Campaign
events.description: Na'im allied with Al Bu Falah ruler Khalifah bin Shakhbut to evict Wahhabis from Buraimi
events.significance: Despite earlier Wahhabi influence, Na'im chose to oppose Saudi expansion

events.title: Expulsion of Wahhabis from Buraimi
events.description: Dhawahir supported Saeed bin Tahnun Al Nahyan in clearing Buraimi Oasis of Wahhabi forces
events.significance: Demonstrated loyalty to Al Nahyan leadership

events.title: Khawatir Adoption of Wahhabism
events.description: The Khawatir adopted Wahhabism as a doctrine in response to encroachment by the Al Bu Falah and their Manasir allies
events.significance: Marked a significant religious and political shift for the tribe

participants.role: Religious and political response to territorial pressure

events.title: Shammar Golden Age
events.description: Shammar ruled much of central and northern Arabia from Riyadh to Syria and Upper Mesopotamia
events.significance: Peak of Shammar power and territorial control

events.title: Death of Maktoum bin Butti
events.description: Died of smallpox while travelling from Muscat to Qeshm
events.significance: End of founding era of Dubai and Al Maktoum dynasty

events.title: Treaty of Perpetual Maritime Peace (1853)
events.description: Extended the 1820 treaty into a permanent arrangement.

events.title: Perpetual Maritime Truce
events.description: By this time, Jazirat Al Hamra had become part of Ras Al Khaimah, and the Zaab had become dependents of the Al Qasimi rulers
events.significance: Marked the loss of independent political status for the Zaab tribe

participants.role: Dependent tribe under Al Qasimi rule

events.title: Killing of Dibba Wali
events.description: The wali of Dibba named Mashari was killed by Shihuh tribesmen
events.significance: Established pattern of rivalry between townsfolk of Dibba and the Shihuh that impacted town revenues

events.title: Defeat by Faisal bin Turki
events.description: The Ajman were defeated by Faisal bin Turki, the Imam of the Second Saudi State, after challenging his rule in the 1850s
events.significance: Major political setback, followed by intermarriage alliance

events.title: First Sharqiyin Rebellion Against Sharjah
events.description: Sharqiyin first withheld tribute to Sharjah on the death of Sultan bin Saqr Al Qasimi, led by Abdullah bin Khamis Al Sharqi
events.significance: Beginning of independence movement

events.title: Second Anti-Wahhabi Campaign
events.description: Na'im allied with Sultan Azzan bin Qais to evict Wahhabis from Buraimi
events.significance: Continued resistance to Saudi influence in the region

events.title: Saudi Withdrawal and Submission to Muscat
events.description: Following the Saudi withdrawal in 1871, the Khawatir submitted to Muscat but later became dependent on Zayed bin Khalifa Al Nahyan
events.significance: Marked transition in political allegiances

events.title: Eviction of Wahhabis from Buraimi
events.description: Despite being linked to Wahhabi influence and adopting the doctrine, the Na'im allied with other forces to evict the Wahhabis from Buraimi
events.significance: After the eviction, they occupied many of the forts around Buraimi

participants.role: The Na'im including Al Bu Shamis allied with other forces to evict the Wahhabis

events.title: Attack on western settlements of Riyadh
events.description: Otaibah chief Muslit bin Rubayan attacked western settlements of Riyadh, leading to retaliatory raid by Saud bin Faisal
events.significance: Part of the complex power struggles in 19th century Najd involving multiple ruling families

events.title: Ottoman Governor Appointment
events.description: Ottomans appointed a governor from Bani Khalid over Al-Hasa
events.significance: Brief and short-lived return to political authority

events.title: Second Sharqiyin Rebellion
events.description: Rebellion triggered when Al Qasimi Wali of Dibba jailed twelve Sharqiyin, resulting in Sharqiyin/Shihu alliance against Sharjah
events.significance: Continued resistance to Sharjah rule

events.title: Dhawahir Rebellion Against Zayed
events.description: The Dhawahir rebelled against Sheikh Zayed bin Khalifah Al Nahyan's policy of buying date groves and water rights, but were defeated after a month-long conflict
events.significance: Led to Zayed taking hostages and consolidating control over Al Ain

events.title: Hamad bin Abdullah's Insurrection
events.description: Sheikh Hamad bin Abdullah Al Sharqi led insurrection against Sheikh Saqr bin Khalid Al Qasimi of Sharjah
events.significance: Major step toward independence

events.title: Abu Dhabi-Qatar War
events.description: The Awamir supported Sheikh Zayed bin Khalifa Al Nahyan in his extended war with Qatar in the 1880s
events.significance: Series of conflicts that secured the western border of Abu Dhabi

participants.role: supported Sheikh Zayed bin Khalifa Al Nahyan

events.title: Property Buyout by Al bu Falah
events.description: In the latter part of the 19th century, the Al bu Falah of Abu Dhabi bought out much of the property previously owned by the Dhawahir
events.significance: Major change in land ownership patterns in Al Ain region oases

events.title: Khedouri Family Settlement in Bahrain
events.description: The Khedouri family arrived in Bahrain as part of the first Jewish settlers in the late 1880s
events.significance: Part of the founding Jewish community in Bahrain

events.title: Raids on Harb camps
events.description: Otaibah plundered camps of Harb tribes who were subjects of Ibn Rashid in 1881 and 1882
events.significance: Continued tribal warfare and resistance against Rashidi authority

events.title: Al Murrah departure from Qatar
events.description: Al Murrah tribespeople, along with Bani Hajer members, renounced allegiance to Qatar and departed the country, later joining with Ajman tribe in opposition
events.significance: Major tribal realignment in Qatar

events.title: Battle between Qatar and opposition tribes
events.description: 1,200 loyalist partisans from Al Murrah and Bani Hajer branches loyal to Qatar's sheikh fought against 2,000 men from the opposition Al Murrah, Bani Hajer and Ajman coalition, inflicting heavy losses despite being outnumbered
events.significance: Demonstrated tribal divisions and Qatar's military capabilities

participants.role: split participation - some loyal to Qatar, others in opposition

events.title: Iran Annexation of Lengeh
events.description: Iran annexed Lengeh, ending Al Qasimi rule on the Persian coast.

events.title: British defeat of Qasimis
events.description: Qasimis were defeated by the British in their 'anti-piracy' campaign and retreated to the southern coast of the Gulf
events.significance: End of Qasimi control in Iranian domains

events.title: Nonoo Family Immigration to Bahrain
events.description: Ibrahim Nonoo left Baghdad and established a financial business in Bahrain
events.significance: Established the Nonoo family presence in Bahrain and began their business legacy

events.title: Mazrui Resistance to British Rule
events.description: When the British East Africa Protectorate was established, the Mazrui were one of the groups that most actively resisted British rule
events.significance: Part of broader East African resistance to colonial rule

events.title: Conquest of 'Ain Dhawahir
events.description: Zayed marched on the oasis with Dubai support and took the Dhawahir's main settlement, building a fort to establish dominion
events.significance: Completed Zayed's consolidation of power in the oasis

events.title: Al Rashid Conquest of Riyadh
events.description: Al Rashid took control of Saudi capital Riyadh and expelled Saudi leaders to Kuwait
events.significance: Shammar-backed Al Rashid temporarily displaced Saudi rule

events.title: Birth of Rouben Mamoulian
events.description: Rouben Zachary Mamoulian born in Tiflis, Russian Empire (now Tbilisi, Georgia) to Armenian family

events.title: Hawar Islands Border Determination
events.description: The Dawasir helped determine the maritime border between Qatar and Bahrain through a tribesman's fishing activities near the Hawar Islands
events.significance: Resulted in one of the longest and most complex cases in international law

events.title: Joining the Ikhwan Movement
events.description: A section of the Ajman led by Dhaydan bin Hithlain joined the Ikhwan movement, providing military support for Ibn Saud
events.significance: Initially supported Ibn Saud's consolidation of power

events.title: Persian Harassment Campaign
events.description: Persian harassment campaign against Arabs of Mohammerah intensified, causing Al-Qarooni/Karuni family to re-migrate
events.significance: Forced migration and displacement of Baharna subgroups

events.title: Fujairah Autonomy
events.description: Sharqiyin tribe repeatedly tried to secede from Sharjah.

events.title: Declaration of Fujairah Independence
events.description: Hamad bin Abdullah declared independence from Sharjah, recognized by all except the British
events.significance: Achievement of de facto independence

events.title: Fujairah Achieves Practical Independence
events.description: The Al Sharqi finally managed to achieve practical independence from Sharjah
events.significance: Marked the beginning of effective Al Sharqi rule over Fujairah

events.title: Wadi Hatta Dispute
events.description: Bani Qitab built a fort at Wadi Hatta and harassed caravans, leading to conflict with Na'im tribe and intervention by Trucial Sheikhs
events.significance: Demonstrated the tribe's strategic control over important trade routes

events.title: Mamoulian Family Migration to Paris
events.description: Family moved to Paris for three years following ethnic violence in Tiflis after Russian Revolution of 1905

events.title: Development of Al Wakrah
events.description: They worked on building up one of Qatar's largest cities, Al Wakrah. J. G. Lorimer noted that the Al-Buainain population in Al Wakrah was 2,000 in 1908.
events.significance: Major contribution to Qatar's urban development

events.title: Abu Dhalouf Village Population Record
events.description: J.G. Lorimer recorded that Abu Dhalouf village was inhabited by 70 families of the Al Mannai tribe
events.significance: Documentation of Al Mannai presence in Qatar

events.title: Ikhwan Settlement Program
events.description: Abdul Aziz Ibn Saud settled nomadic tribes including Mutayr in hijras (villages) to indoctrinate them into puritanical Islam
events.significance: Transformed Mutayr from nomadic tribe to settled warriors supporting Ibn Saud's cause

events.title: Battle of Kinzan
events.description: Battle in Al Ahsa'a where King Abdulaziz Al Saud fought against Al Ajman tribe to stop their attacks on civilians
events.significance: Earned Bani Hajer the title 'Khaznat Thafar' (Vault of Victory) and land grants from King Abdulaziz

participants.role: King Abdulaziz's ally - Bani Hajer under Sheikh Shafi bin Salim Al Sahfi joined the battle and turned the tide in the King's favor

events.title: Kurdish Migration During WWI
events.description: Kurdish migration from southeastern Turkey to avoid violence and turmoil of World War I
events.significance: Added Kurdish component to the Ajam community

events.title: Arab Revolt against Ottomans
events.description: The Ruwallah were among the tribes that took part in the 'Arab Revolt' against the Ottomans
events.significance: Demonstrated Anizah involvement in Arab nationalism movement

participants.role: The Ruwallah branch participated in the revolt

events.title: Flight from Russia
events.description: The Mamoulian family, sympathetic to the Czarist regime, fled Russia during the turmoil of the 1917 revolution and ensuing civil war

events.title: Establishment of Al Islah District
events.description: Shaikh Sulaiman bin Mansur Al Nasrallah established the district of Al Islah in Dhi Qar
events.significance: Created the administrative center for settled branches of Albu Salih in their traditional marshland territory

events.title: Attack on Kuwait at al-Jahra
events.description: Al-Dewish led an attack by the Ikhwan on Kuwait but was compelled to withdraw under British pressure
events.significance: Failed military campaign that demonstrated British influence in the region

events.title: Integration into Trucial State of Ras Al Khaimah
events.description: Rams and Sha'am became part of the recognised Trucial State of Ras Al Khaimah
events.significance: Marked the end of the fractious relationship and formal political integration

participants.role: End of secession period and formal integration into the state structure

events.title: Sha'am becomes part of Trucial State of Ras Al Khaimah
events.description: The village of Sha'am, originally settled entirely by the Bani Shatair, seceded several times before becoming part of the recognised Trucial State of Ras Al Khaimah in 1921
events.significance: Marked the end of fractious relationship between Shihhu and Rulers of Ras Al Khaimah regarding Sha'am

participants.role: Primary inhabitants of Sha'am village during this political transition

events.title: The Battle for Rams
events.description: Fighting broke out in June 1921 between Sheikh Sultan bin Salim Al Qasimi of Ras Al Khaimah and the Shihuh who had allied with headman Abdelrahman bin Saleh Al Tanaiji of Rams
events.significance: Led to British intervention and multiple treaty negotiations, disrupted pearling season

participants.role: Allied with local headman against Ras Al Khaimah rule

events.title: Conquest of Ha'il
events.description: Ibn Saud and Wahhabi forces defeated Al Rashid, ending Shammar rule in Ha'il
events.significance: End of Shammar political dominance in Arabian Peninsula, led to migration to Syria and Iraq

events.title: Umm Al Qawain Succession Crisis
events.description: After Sheikh Rashid bin Ahmad Al Mualla died of pneumonia, Bani Qitab under Sheikh Muhammed bin Ali Al Huwaidan helped secure succession of Sheikh Abdulla bin Rashid Al Mualla II
events.significance: Showed the tribe's political influence and loyalty to allied rulers

events.title: Founding of Dammam and Khobar
events.description: The cities of Dammam and Khobar, in Eastern Saudi Arabia, were founded in 1923 by the Dawasir tribe that migrated from Bahrain after King AbdulAziz allowed them to settle within the area
events.significance: Established major urban centers in Eastern Saudi Arabia

events.title: Bahrain Exile and Return
events.description: The Dawasir tribe opposed the British overthrow of Sheikh Isa ibn Ali Al Khalifa in 1923 and left Bahrain, returning in April 1927
events.significance: Demonstrated tribal political power and resistance to colonial interference

events.title: Khalid bin Ahmad Al Qasimi Deposition
events.description: When Ruler of Sharjah Khalid bin Ahmad Al Qasimi was deposed, Bani Qitab sided with Khalid against the new Ruler Sultan
events.significance: Demonstrated the tribe's involvement in succession disputes

events.title: Battle of Qaysar
events.description: Ikhwan forces from Mutair tribe attacked Al-Dhafeer in Qaysar region of southern Iraq on May 31, 1924
events.significance: Al-Dhafeer victory killing 72 Ikhwan fighters including notable leader Haif bin Shuqair al-Duwaish

events.title: Conquest of Hejaz
events.description: Mutayri contingent led by Al-Dewish joined the Ikhwan conquest of Hejaz on behalf of Ibn Saud
events.significance: Successful military campaign that expanded Saudi control

events.title: British Bombardment of Fujairah Fort
events.description: British bombarded Fujairah Fort after Hamad bin Abdullah refused to board British ship regarding slave trading
events.significance: Conflict with British over slave trade embargo

events.title: Destruction of Dhu l-Khalasah
events.description: King Abdulaziz ordered destruction of the ancient idol Dhu l-Khalasah worshiped by local clans
events.significance: Marked end of pre-Islamic religious practices in the region during Saudi state formation

events.title: Dibba Treaty with Shihuh
events.description: The wali of Dibba signed a treaty with the Shihuh which broke down on his death in 1932
events.significance: Temporary peace that collapsed leading to appeal to Muscat for protection

events.title: Kalba Resettlement Proposal
events.description: British proposal to rehouse the Shihuh at Kalba and give them control of the Shamailiyah to reduce conflicts
events.significance: Showed British frustration with conflicts but proposal came to nothing

events.title: Dhaid Water Dispute Victory
events.description: The Khawatir prevailed in disputes with Bani Qitab neighbors in Dhaid, with suspicions of water supply interference due to poor date crop
events.significance: Established Khawatir dominance in Dhaid area

events.title: Dhaid Settlement and Political Alliance
events.description: Settlement conducted by Na'im chiefs led to Khalid bin Ahmed Al Qasimi's ascension to rule Dhaid, followed by Khawatir support for his attempted invasion of Sharjah
events.significance: Demonstrated Khawatir political influence and alliances

events.title: Battle of Sabilla
events.description: Ibn Saud defeated the Ikhwan rebels led by Al-Dewish in northeastern Nejd
events.significance: Decisive defeat that ended the Ikhwan rebellion and led to Al-Dewish's capture and death

events.title: Battle of Sabilla
events.description: The Ajman and their allies from the tribes of Utaybah and Mutayr were defeated by Ibn Saud, ending the Ikhwan rebellion
events.significance: Put an end to the Ikhwan rebellion and Ajman political independence

events.title: US Naturalization
events.description: Mamoulian became a naturalized citizen of the United States

events.title: Mass Huwala return migration
events.description: Imposition of restrictive economic policies by Reza Shah led to migration of most remaining Huwala back to Arabian Peninsula
events.significance: Final major exodus from Iran to Arabian Peninsula

events.title: Abraham Nonoo Municipal Service
events.description: Abraham Nonoo served as member of the Manama Municipality
events.significance: First political involvement of the Nonoo family in Bahrain

events.title: Khalid bin Ahmad's Accession as Ruler of Kalba
events.description: Bani Qitab played a key role in supporting Khalid bin Ahmad Al Qasimi's accession as Ruler of Kalba
events.significance: Further demonstrated the tribe's kingmaking influence in the region

events.title: Qatar Na'im Split
events.description: Na'im in Qatar split between Ramzan (supporting Qatar) and Al Jabr (supporting Bahrain) over Zubarah dispute
events.significance: Demonstrates how external political pressures divided tribal loyalties

events.title: Kalba Independence from Sharjah
events.description: Kalba became independent of Sharjah with the accession of Khalid bin Ahmad Al Qasimi as Ruler
events.significance: Demonstrated the political influence of the Naqbiyin in regional affairs

participants.role: The Naqbiyin were the 'powers behind the throne' supporting this independence

events.title: Marriage Alliance with Al Thani
events.description: Emir Abdullah bin Jassim Al Thani's first wife was from the Al Mannai tribe
events.significance: Political connection between Al Mannai and ruling Al Thani family

events.title: Saudi Alliance
events.description: A century later the Bani Kaab were in alliance with the Saudis, with Sheikh Obaid bin Jumah confirming to the Governor of Saudi Arabia's Eastern Province, 'Our territories are yours.'
events.significance: Shift in tribal allegiances

events.title: Opposition to Oil Concessions
events.description: In the 1940s the Bani Kaab came together with the Na'im when oil concessions loomed, opposing the oil companies, the Trucial Sheikhs, the Sultan of Muscat and the British
events.significance: Resistance to foreign economic control

events.title: Opposition to Oil Exploration
events.description: The Ghafalah were one of a number of tribes opposed to oil exploration taking place inland of the Trucial States, particularly in the late 1940s, frequently allying with the Khawatir
events.significance: Frustrated coastal Qawasim rulers in granting oil concessions and access to the interior

events.title: Al Khulaifat District First Mapped
events.description: The Al Khulaifat district, named after the local Al Khulaifat tribe, first appeared in maps around the 1940s
events.significance: Marks the formal recognition of the district named after the tribe

events.title: End of Nomadic Lifestyle
events.description: Majority of Albu Salih nomads abandoned nomadic lifestyle and settled permanently
events.significance: Marked the end of traditional seasonal migration patterns and transition to settled life

events.title: Dubai-Abu Dhabi War
events.description: Awamir raiding led to open and bloody warfare between tribes and the 1946 war between Dubai and Abu Dhabi
events.significance: First time British were forced to intervene in interior matters and broker agreements

participants.role: their raiding activities contributed to the conflict

events.title: Incorporation of Masfout into Ajman
events.description: Masfout was seized from Na'im Sheikh Saqr bin Sultan Al Hamouda by Sheikh Rashid bin Humaid Al Nuaimi III of Ajman
events.significance: End of Na'im independence in Masfout, now an Ajman exclave

events.title: Displacement from Hula Valley
events.description: Al Fadl displaced from homes in Hula Valley during Arab-Israeli war
events.significance: Major population displacement affecting tribal structure

events.title: First Baloch Insurgency
events.description: Insurgency by Baloch nationalists from 1948-1950
events.significance: First major post-independence resistance movement

events.title: Bani Yas Population Decline
events.description: Population fell from 12,000 to approximately 8,000 due to emigration for employment
events.significance: Major demographic shift due to economic changes before oil boom

events.title: Buraimi Dispute
events.description: The Bani Kaab supported the Saudi incursion under Shaikh Rashid bin Hamad of the Al Bu Shamis and were involved in fighting against the British force, the Trucial Oman Levies
events.significance: Major territorial dispute in the region

events.title: Buraimi Crisis
events.description: Border conflict involving Saudi Arabia, Oman, and UAE concerning the Buraimi Governorate
events.significance: End of Al Nuaimi rule in Al Buraimi

events.title: Omani Territorial Claims Dispute
events.description: Wali of Bukha claimed Habus areas as Omani territory, leading to fighting in Sha'am
events.significance: Led to formal recognition of Al Qasimi sovereignty in 1955

events.title: Saqr bin Sultan Al Qasimi becomes ruler of Sharjah
events.description: Saqr acceded to rule Sharjah on the death of his father Sultan bin Saqr Al Qasimi II in May 1951
events.significance: Beginning of Saqr's rule which would be marked by Arab nationalist policies

events.title: Fujairah British Recognition
events.description: Fujairah finally gained British recognition as a Trucial State — last to be recognized.

events.title: British Recognition of Fujairah as Trucial State
events.description: British formally recognized Fujairah as a Trucial State
events.significance: Formal international recognition of independence

events.title: British Recognition of Fujairah as Trucial State
events.description: Britain formally recognized Fujairah as a separate Trucial State under Al Sharqi rule
events.significance: Provided international legitimacy to Al Sharqi rule

events.title: Recognition of Al Qasimi Sovereignty
events.description: Habus finally recognized Al Qasimi sovereignty after years of semi-autonomous status
events.significance: Ended period of disputed sovereignty but tribe continued to be troublesome subjects

events.title: Sudanese Independence
events.description: On 1 January 1956, Sudan was declared an independent state
events.significance: End of colonial rule and beginning of modern Sudanese state

events.title: Establishment of Al Nahyaneia Model School
events.description: Sheikh Zayed Bin Sultan Al Nahyan established the first formal school in Al Ain
events.significance: Marked the beginning of modernization of the education system in the U.A.E.

events.title: Arab League delegation visits Trucial States
events.description: Arab League delegation headed by Abdel Khaleq Hassouna proposed £5 million development fund for Trucial States
events.significance: Key moment that heightened tensions between Arab nationalist rulers and British administrators

events.title: Deposition of Saqr bin Sultan Al Qasimi
events.description: British officials led by Terence Clark deposed Saqr in a bloodless palace coup using Trucial Oman Scouts
events.significance: Marked British response to Arab nationalist influence in the Trucial States

events.title: Accession as Ruler of Sharjah
events.description: Became ruler following exile of cousin Saqr bin Sultan Al Qasimi, confirmed by British Political Resident William Luce
events.significance: Established legitimate rule with British backing after family consensus

events.title: Demolition of Sharjah Fort
events.description: Ordered demolition of Sharjah Fort to erase memory of predecessor Saqr, stopped by brother Sultan
events.significance: Symbolic attempt to break with previous rule, later restored by his brother

events.title: End of Mahra Sultanate
events.description: The Mahra Sultanate that lasted five centuries came to an end
events.significance: Marked the end of Mahri political autonomy after centuries of rule

events.title: Displacement from Golan Heights
events.description: Al Fadl displaced from Golan Heights during Arab-Israeli war, most settled around Damascus
events.significance: Further displacement leading to concentration in Damascus area

events.title: Federation Proposal (1968-1971)
events.description: Nine sheikhdoms considered forming one federation. Bahrain and Qatar withdrew.

events.title: Mass Migration to Abu Dhabi
events.description: Following disputes with Sheikh Saqr bin Mohammed Al Qasimi of Ras Al Khaimah, the majority of the Zaab tribe accepted Sheikh Zayed's offer to relocate to Abu Dhabi
events.significance: Resulted in the abandonment of their ancestral village and establishment of Al Zaab area in Abu Dhabi

events.title: Iran's Seizure of Tunb Islands (1971)
events.description: Iran seized the Greater and Lesser Tunb islands. Convinced RAK to join the UAE.

events.title: UAE Foundation Negotiations
events.description: Key participant in negotiations leading to UAE formation on 2 December 1971
events.significance: Became one of the founding fathers of the United Arab Emirates

events.title: Ras Al Khaimah Joins UAE
events.description: RAK was the last emirate to join the UAE on February 10, 1972.

events.title: Kalba-Fujairah Land Dispute
events.description: Land dispute between Kalba and Fujairah that escalated to open fighting, killing 22 and injuring a dozen more before UAE Union Defence Force intervention
events.significance: Required mediation by Sheikh Rashid of Dubai and other rulers to resolve

participants.role: Participants in the conflict as residents of Kalba

events.title: Land Dispute with Kalba
events.description: Land dispute broke out into open fighting, requiring Union Defence Force intervention, killing 22 and injuring dozen more
events.significance: Post-union territorial conflict

events.title: Failed coup attempt by Saqr bin Sultan
events.description: Saqr returned from Egypt with mercenaries and attempted to seize power, resulting in the murder of Khalid bin Muhammad Al Qasimi
events.significance: Tragic event that marked the end of Saqr's political ambitions and resulted in the death of a UAE founding father

events.title: Death in Coup Attempt
events.description: Killed during coup attempt by former ruler Saqr bin Sultan Al Qasimi on 24-25 January 1972
events.significance: Died defending his rule against mercenary-backed coup attempt

events.title: 1972 Sharjah Coup
events.description: Former ruler Saqr bin Sultan Al Qasimi attempted to regain control of Sharjah from his cousin Khalid bin Muhammad Al Qasimi with Egyptian mercenaries. Despite initially seizing the palace and killing Khalid, Saqr was forced to surrender to the UAE Union Defence Force.
events.significance: First major internal conflict in the newly formed UAE, demonstrated the effectiveness of federal intervention and the Union Defence Force
events.outcome: Failed coup - Saqr surrendered to UAE forces and was imprisoned until 1979
events.surpriseFactor: Moderate - Saqr had been in exile since 1965 and was known to be disaffected with Khalid's policies regarding Iranian occupation of UAE islands

participants.action: Led coup attempt with Egyptian mercenaries to regain throne
Defended palace but was killed during the coup attempt
Led Union Defence Force response and accepted Saqr's surrender

events.title: 1972 Qatar Coup
events.description: A bloodless palace coup that took place in Qatar on 22 February 1972, carried out by heir apparent and prime minister Khalifa bin Hamad Al Thani against his cousin, Emir Ahmad bin Ali Al Thani, while the latter was visiting Iran
events.significance: Marked a shift in Qatar's political structure with centralization of power under the new Emir and reduction of traditional family privileges
events.outcome: Successful transfer of power from Ahmad bin Ali Al Thani to Khalifa bin Hamad Al Thani, who became the new Emir and reorganized the government, reducing traditional powers of heir apparent and limiting financial privileges of Al Thani family members
events.surpriseFactor: High - executed while the Emir was out of country on diplomatic visit to Iran

participants.action: Led the bloodless palace coup as heir apparent and prime minister, took control of the country and became new Emir
Was deposed as Emir while on diplomatic visit to Iran, later lived in exile in London until death in 1977
Appointed as Minister of Foreign Affairs by the new Emir after the coup
Had their financial privileges limited by the new Emir as part of government reorganization

events.title: 1979 seizure of the Grand Mosque of Mecca
events.description: Muhammad al-Qahtani, claiming to be the Mahdi, led the seizure of the Grand Mosque
events.significance: Major religious and political incident in modern Saudi history

participants.role: A tribe member (Muhammad al-Qahtani) led this significant religious-political event

events.title: Wedad Al Kuwari begins writing career
events.description: Started writing for local magazines while in high school and joined Qatar TV's script department
events.significance: Beginning of influential career in Gulf media

events.title: Sheikh Zayed visits Bangladesh
events.description: Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan visited Bangladesh in May 1984 and expressed desire to establish orphanages
events.significance: Led to establishment of charitable trust for orphanages

events.title: Construction of Al Qasimi Palace
events.description: Sheikh Abdulaziz bin Humaid Al Qasimi built a 4-story palace in Ras Al Khaimah, which was completed in 1985 but abandoned after one night due to alleged hauntings
events.significance: Became known as the 'Haunted Palace' of Ras Al Khaimah and later converted to a heritage museum

events.title: Lost TV Series
events.description: Wedad Al Kuwari wrote the Qatari series 'Lost'
events.significance: Early major television work

events.title: Establishment of Sheikh Zayed Trust Bangladesh
events.description: Founded the Sheikh Zayed Bin Sultan al Nahyan Trust Bangladesh to run orphanages with initial funding of US$50 thousand
events.significance: Created lasting institutional framework for UAE charitable work in Bangladesh

events.title: 1987 Sharjah Coup Attempt
events.description: A political crisis in the United Arab Emirates when Sheikh Abd al-Aziz bin Muhammad Al Qasimi attempted a bloodless palace coup against his brother Sultan bin Muhammad Al-Qasimi, Sheikh of Sharjah, seizing key positions throughout Sharjah with military elements loyal to him due to economic recession from the 1980s oil glut.
events.significance: The first coup in the Persian Gulf region since the 1973 oil crisis, it destabilized the foundation of the young UAE and threatened to break the union back into its constituent Emirates, but ultimately strengthened federal unity
events.outcome: Failed - Abd al-Aziz surrendered on June 24, 1987, in exchange for amnesty and being named deputy emir/crown prince
events.surpriseFactor: High - described as a 'generation defining political event' that caught international attention and raised fears of UAE dissolution

participants.action: Led the coup attempt, seized the Emir's court with loyal military forces
Was the sitting Sheikh of Sharjah who was deposed during the coup
UAE Minister of Defense who negotiated the settlement and convinced other Emirati leaders to reinstall Sultan
Sheikh of Abu Dhabi and UAE President who initially seemed to support the coup but later backed off
Ruler of Ras Al Khaimah who supported the coup attempt to merge both branches of Al Qasimi
Led committee investigating the cause of the coup

events.title: Death of Abdul Ali Mazari
events.description: Death of Afghan Hazara leader Abdul Ali Mazari, which later influenced revenge killings in Mazar-i-Sharif
events.significance: This death later motivated revenge killings of Taliban prisoners in 1997

events.title: 1995 Qatar Coup
events.description: A bloodless palace coup carried out by Crown Prince Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani who took control of Qatar while his father Emir Khalifa bin Hamad Al Thani was visiting Geneva, Switzerland on 27 June 1995. The coup occurred after a falling-out between father and son over authority that the father had tried to regain in early 1995.
events.significance: Major political transition in Qatar's ruling Al Thani family. Led to modernization and reform under new leadership. Strained relations with traditional Arab allies who were implicated in counter-coup attempt.
events.outcome: Successful coup. Hamad bin Khalifa became Emir. Former Emir lived in exile until 2004. Failed counter-coup attempt in February 1996.
events.surpriseFactor: Moderate - occurred during father's absence abroad, but tensions had been building since early 1995

participants.action: Led bloodless palace coup while serving as Crown Prince, took control of country
Was deposed while on visit to Geneva, lived in exile until 2004
Provided support to Hamad bin Khalifa during the coup
Led failed counter-coup attempt in February 1996 as former Economy Minister

events.title: Failed coup attempt against Sheikh Hamad Al Thani
events.description: 119 Al Murrah members attempted to overthrow Sheikh Hamad Al Thani eight months after he deposed his father Sheikh Khalifa Al Thani in 1995. 19 were sentenced to death, 33 to life imprisonment
events.significance: Major political crisis in modern Qatar

events.title: Inherited Basma Company
events.description: Returned to Bahrain after father's death to take over the Basma Company, offering various office services
events.significance: Became successful businesswoman continuing family business tradition

events.title: Darfur Genocide
events.description: Ethnic genocide in Darfur region committed by Omar al-Bashir's regime from 2003–2020
events.significance: Resulted in deaths of 300,000 to 400,000 people and international condemnation

events.title: Copyright dispute with Fajer Al-Saeed
events.description: Dispute over rights to screenplay 'Another Day' involving similarities with 'Al Hareem'
events.significance: Notable legal case in Gulf media industry

events.title: Modern Baloch Insurgency
events.description: New ongoing insurgency by autonomy-seeking Baloch groups since 2003
events.significance: Current autonomy movement challenging Pakistani state control

events.title: Founded Bahrain Human Rights Watch Society
events.description: Founded and presided over the Bahrain Human Rights Watch Society for advancement of women's rights and foreign workers' rights
events.significance: Advocacy work for human rights in Bahrain

events.title: Merowe Dam Impact on Dar al-Manasir
events.description: The Merowe Dam construction affected all villages of Dar al-Manasir, leading to relocation issues for the tribe.
events.significance: Major displacement event requiring relocation of entire homeland

events.title: Appointment to Majlis al-shura
events.description: Appointed by King Hamad ibn Isa Al Khalifah to serve as member of the 40-member Shura Council
events.significance: Notable appointment of Jewish community member to Bahraini parliament

events.title: First Tribal Chief Election
events.description: Members of Zahran tribe elected their tribal chief Mohammad Bin Yahya Al Zahrani
events.significance: First election of its kind in Saudi Arabia, showing modernization of tribal governance

events.title: Publication of 'From Our Beginning to Present Day'
events.description: Nancy Khedouri documented the history of Bahraini Jews from first settlers to present day
events.significance: First comprehensive documentation of Bahraini Jewish community history

events.title: Houda Nonoo Ambassador Appointment
events.description: Houda Nonoo appointed as Bahraini Ambassador to the United States
events.significance: Historic appointment as first Jewish ambassador of Bahrain and any Middle Eastern Arab country

events.title: Appointment as Ambassador to the United States
events.description: Appointed as Bahraini Ambassador to the United States by Foreign Affairs Minister Khalid bin Ahmed Al Khalifa on July 3, 2008
events.significance: Historic appointment as first Jewish and first female Bahraini ambassador to the US

events.title: Hearts for Rent controversy
events.description: Controversial Qatari soap opera about family displacement due to high rent prices
events.significance: Sparked social debate about housing issues in Qatar despite winning Best Screenplay Award

events.title: 2010 Bahraini Parliamentary Election
events.description: Mohamed Albuflasa stood as independent candidate calling for change, later withdrew in favor of Al-Menbar Islamic Society candidate
events.significance: Demonstrated political engagement and criticism of parliamentary dysfunction

events.title: Naming of Al Maktoum International Airport
events.description: The airport was eventually named the Al Maktoum International Airport after the House of Maktoum which rules the Emirate of Dubai
events.significance: Recognition of the ruling family's status in Dubai through naming of major infrastructure

events.title: Nancy Khedouri Joins National Assembly
events.description: Nancy Khedouri became member of Bahrain's National Assembly
events.significance: Landmark appointment of Jewish woman to Bahraini parliament

events.title: South Sudan Independence
events.description: Independence of South Sudan from Sudan
events.significance: Result of long civil wars between Islamic North and Christian/Animist South

events.title: Pearl Roundabout Speech by Mohamed Albuflasa
events.description: Mohamed Albuflasa addressed Bahraini protesters calling for political reform and sectarian unity
events.significance: Significant as a Sunni Salafist supporting mainly Shia-led protests, promoting cross-sectarian unity in Bahrain

events.title: Pearl Roundabout Speech
events.description: Mohamed Albuflasa addressed protesters at Pearl Roundabout during Bahraini uprising, calling for political reform and unity between Shia and Sunni
events.significance: First political prisoner of Bahraini uprising; significant as religiously conservative Sunni supporting mostly Shia protesters

events.title: End of Ambassador Term
events.description: Role as ambassador ended in November 2013, replaced by Abdullah bin Mohammad bin Rashed Al Khalifa
events.significance: Completion of historic five-year diplomatic tenure

events.title: Establishment of Al Qasimia University
events.description: Sheikh Dr. Sultan Bin Mohammed Al Qasimi established Al Qasimia University in Sharjah
events.significance: Created a national university serving the educational needs of the UAE

events.title: Citizenship revocation by Qatar
events.description: Qatar revoked citizenship of the Al Murrah sheikh and 54 tribespeople
events.significance: Continuation of political tensions between Al Murrah and Qatari government

events.title: Fall of Omar al-Bashir
events.description: Protests in 2018 demanding Bashir's resignation resulted in a coup d'état on 11 April 2019 and Bashir's imprisonment
events.significance: End of 30-year military dictatorship

events.title: Qatar 2022 FIFA World Cup Bid
events.description: Al-Thawadi served as deputy chief executive for Qatar's successful bid to host the FIFA World Cup
events.significance: Major diplomatic and international relations achievement for Qatar

events.title: Approval of Al Maktoum Airport Major Expansion
events.description: Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid al-Maktoum approved a major expansion of Al Maktoum International Airport with construction of a new 128 billion AED ($34.85 billion USD) passenger terminal
events.significance: Major infrastructure investment decision that will create the world's largest airport

events.title: Gaza Executive Board Appointment
events.description: Al-Thawadi was appointed as the Qatari representative to the Gaza Executive Board
events.significance: Continues family's involvement in Gaza affairs

events.title: Gaza War Mediation
events.description: Al-Thawadi was involved in negotiations between Hamas and Israel leading to ceasefire and hostage releases
events.significance: Demonstrates Qatar's and Al-Thawadi family's role in international conflict resolution
